---
title: "County ILI Surveillance Report"
output: pdf_document
params:
  county_name: "Unknown County"
  county_summary: NULL
  county_weekly: NULL
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)

county_summary <- params$county_summary
county_weekly  <- params$county_weekly
```

# `r params$county_name` County — Influenza-Like Illness Surveillance Report

**Report generated:** `r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`

## Summary

```{r}
knitr::kable(
  county_summary %>%
    select(
      Population               = population,
      `Latest Week Cases`      = latest_cases,
      `Case Rate per 100k`     = case_rate_per_100k,
      `Z-score`                = z_score,
      Priority                 = priority
    ),
  align = "c"
)
```

## Weekly Case Trend

```{r fig.width=6, fig.height=3.5}
ggplot(county_weekly, aes(x = week, y = cases)) +
  geom_line(color = "#1f77b4", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#1f77b4", size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Epi Week",
    y = "Reported Cases",
    title = paste(params$county_name, "Weekly ILI Cases")
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Recommendation

```{r results='asis'}
priority <- county_summary$priority[1]

recommendation_text <- case_when(
  priority == "High - deploy resources now" ~
    "This county is showing a statistically significant spike in cases relative to its recent baseline. Immediate deployment of additional surveillance and response resources is recommended.",
  priority == "Medium - increase monitoring" ~
    "This county is showing an elevated but not yet critical deviation from baseline. Increased monitoring frequency is recommended.",
  priority == "Low - routine surveillance" ~
    "This county's case counts are within the expected range. Continue routine surveillance.",
  TRUE ~
    "A data quality issue was detected in this county's most recent report. Follow up with local reporting staff to confirm the figures before drawing conclusions."
)

cat(recommendation_text)
```

---

*This report was generated automatically from state ILI surveillance data. For questions, contact the state epidemiology office.*
